entries.definition:
h–n–n moaning sound of a she-camel longing for her young, yearning, mercy, kindness, compassion, wife. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the Qur’an: حَنَان hanān; and حُنَيْن ḥunayn.
حَنَان hanān [v. n./n.] mercy, compassion, tenderness (19:12–13) وَآتَيْنَاهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا وَحَنَانًا مِنْ لَدُنَّا وَزَكَاةً وَكَانَ تَقِيًّا while he was still a boy, We granted him wisdom, tenderness from Us, and purity, he was devout.

حُنَيْن Hunayn [proper name] name of a valley nearly three miles from Mecca where the Muslims fought a battle in 8 A.H./630 A.D. with their opponents (9:25) وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنْكُمْ شَيْئًا and on the day of the battle of Hunayn, when you were well pleased with your multitude, but they availed you nothing.
